mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding aggregate income and consumption, driving corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not positive in greater than 50% of months over a 3-year window, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending forecasts and generate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal Rank IC is not negative at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contracting while the federal funds rate is below 1% reflects monetary cushioning of a supply contraction, which reduces recession risk and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of these conditions coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of episodes over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, increases in housing starts reflect credit-stimulated durable demand that reliably boosts economically sensitive sectors and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases during periods with FEDFUNDS below 1% fail to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals aggregate demand expansion that translates into positive earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises do not correlate with positive 60-day returns, yielding a Rank IC below 0.02 over a 10-year history.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer prices captured by a 3-month annualized CPIAUCSL change above 3% signals impending monetary tightening or margin compression, reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes coinciding with negative signal produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 4% but nonfarm payrolls print a negative monthly change, the interaction captures late-cycle labor market saturation that precedes rapid equity drawdowns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4% and monthly PAYEMS change negative
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of triggered weeks during a 5-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contraction in industrial activity that reduces corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Monthly INDPRO declines coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the directional accuracy falls below 45% over a 3-year window.
